headline: Municipal councils collected RM2.1 billion but 40% went to salaries
context: Malaysia's 155 local authorities collected RM2.1 billion in assessment taxes in 2025, with 40% absorbed by staff salaries and only 31% reaching infrastructure and services.

cards.big: 40% of council taxes go to council salaries — only 31% reaches actual services
cards.sub: Local governments spending more on themselves than on residents they serve

cards.big: Local Government Act 1976 sets no maximum ratio for administrative to service spending
cards.sub: Councils have no legal obligation to prioritise service delivery over internal costs

cards.big: DBKL alone spent RM410 million on emoluments against RM280 million on infrastructure
cards.sub: Malaysia's largest council exemplifies the administrative cost problem

cards.big: Without elected councillors, residents cannot vote out underperforming local administrations
cards.sub: Appointed councillors face no electoral consequence for poor service delivery

cards.big: Local government that spends more on itself than on residents has its priorities exactly backwards
Should local authorities be required to spend at least 50% of revenue on services?
